The phrase "the user must select 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation'" translates to the disjunction 'newPatient | resumeVentilation'. The phrase "the system transitions to self-test mode" maps to 'SelfTestMode'. The construct "X must happen before Y" is captured by the 'until' operator, specifically '!Y U X', meaning Y must not occur until X occurs, and X will eventually occur. Therefore, "the user must select 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' before the system transitions to self-test mode" translates to '!(SelfTestMode) U (newPatient | resumeVentilation)'. Finally, "Once [condition A] [condition B must hold]" is a global requirement that whenever condition A is met, condition B must subsequently hold. explanation: The phrase "Once the start-up has been completed successfully" maps to the atomic proposition 'startUpDone'. This signifies a point in time from which a subsequent condition must hold globally. So, we'll have an 'F (startUpDone & G (...))' structure. The user's action "select 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation'" translates to the disjunction 'newPatient | resumeVentilation'. The system's action "transitions to self-test mode" maps to 'SelfTestMode'. The core temporal constraint is that the user's selection must happen "before" the system transitions to self-test mode. The pattern 'A must happen before B' can be expressed as '! (B & (!A U B))', meaning it's globally not the case that B is true while A has not occurred yet (A being false until B becomes true). Combining these, the formula states that eventually, 'startUpDone' becomes true, and from that point onwards, it is globally true that 'SelfTestMode' cannot be true if '(newPatient | resumeVentilation)' has not been selected yet.
